note: Our features (IsPhoneNumber vs IsHash) are unrelated additive classes in the same two files, so full file-level disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: both files stay shared, but we each insert only in alphabetical-order locations to avoid line collisions: in _other.py, add each class as its own new class block keeping the file's existing alphabetical class order (IsHash before IsPhoneNumber); in __init__.py, add each name to the existing import-from-_other list and __all__ list in alphabetical order (IsHash before IsPhoneNumber). Since these are independent append/insert operations in sorted lists, they shouldn't clobber each other's lines. Please confirm ACCEPT if this works for you, then we'll each implement independently.
